question.text: Which of the following is NOT classified as a Qualified Institutional Buyer (QIB) under SEBI ICDR Regulations?

answer.text: Foreign portfolio investors who are individuals or family offices

explanation.text: **The Logic:** While FPIs are generally classified as QIBs, the regulations specifically EXCLUDE individuals, corporates, and family offices registered as FPIs from QIB status. Only institutional category FPIs qualify as QIBs. **The Trap:** Candidates often assume all FPIs are QIBs without reading the exclusion clause. SEBI-registered mutual funds, SCBs, and IRDAI-registered insurance companies are all clearly listed as QIBs. **Cross-Exam Context:** NISM Series V-B (Mutual Fund) and NISM Series XII (Securities Markets Foundation) also test QIB classification for allocation purposes. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** Remember the FPI exclusion — "not all FPIs are QIBs." Individual/corporate/family office FPIs are excluded from QIB status despite being FPIs.
